summary: Recent updates to the WebRTC web API have improved the overall performance and functionality of video calls and other real-time communication features in Firefox. A key addition is the implementation of **getRemoteCertificates**, which allows for enhanced security by enabling the retrieval of remote certificates used for encryption, ensuring a safer connection. The changes also included enhancements in memory management by adopting modern C++ practices, like using `std::make_unique` and `MakeRefPtr`, which help prevent memory leaks and improve performance. Finally, enabling transport-cc for audio codecs allows for better handling of network conditions, improving call quality by dynamically adjusting audio data transmission based on the state of the network.
